sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Parma and Como is set for May 3, 2025, at 13:00, in the iconic Ennio Tardini stadium in Parma. This Round 35 fixture sees Parma, currently 15th in the league with 32 points, hosting Como, who sit in 11th place with 42 points. With both teams separated by 10 points, this match holds significant importance for Parma to close the gap and for Como to solidify their mid-table standing. The referee, Marco Di Bello, is known for a strict approach, averaging 4.8 yellow cards and 0.28 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially fiery encounter. Expect a competitive battle as both sides look to gain crucial points late in the season.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: Looking at their past encounters, the last matchup on October 19, 2024, ended in a 1-1 draw with Parma’s Adrián Bernabé earning Player of the Match honors with a 7.6 Sofascore rating. Over the last 5 head-to-head games, Como has secured 2 wins, Parma has 1 win, and 2 matches ended in draws, indicating a tightly contested rivalry. Notably, in 4 of their last 5 meetings, the total goals have been under 2.5, suggesting low-scoring affairs. Both teams have scored in 6 of their last 8 encounters, and Parma has been the first to score in 5 of the last 6 games against Como. Hernani stands out for Parma in these matchups, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.38, with 1 goal and 3 assists in 4 starts against Como.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Como enters this match in impressive form, riding a 4-game winning streak and remaining unbeaten in their last 5 away games, with 3 consecutive clean sheets on the road. They’ve also been the first to score in 9 of their last 10 matches, showcasing their ability to start strong. Parma, on the other hand, is unbeaten in their last 7 home games, which could provide a solid foundation for a positive result. However, Parma has failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 home games against Como, while Como has struggled to do the same in their last 3 away games against Parma. Both teams have seen under 10.5 corners in recent matches, with Parma at 5 of their last 6 home games and Como at 8 of their last 10 away games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Parma, Enrico Del Prato has been a defensive standout with a Sofascore rating of 7.01 and 4 goals, overperforming his expected goals by 1.9397. Up front, Ange-Yoan Bonny leads with 6 goals, though underperforming his 8.98 expected goals by nearly 3. Hernani, with a 6.91 rating, adds creativity with 2 assists. Como boasts Nico Paz, a midfield dynamo with a league rank of 2 for successful dribbles per game at 2.1, alongside 6 goals and 6 assists, despite underperforming his 8.49 expected goals. Patrick Cutrone matches Paz with 6 goals and adds 4 assists, while Gabriel Strefezza contributes with 4 goals and 4 assists. These players will be pivotal in deciding the outcome.

sections.subtitle: Notable Absences and Impact
sections.text: Parma will miss key midfielder Adrián Bernabé, who has a high Sofascore rating of 7.14, along with forward Matteo Cancellieri, who has 3 goals this season. Nahuel Estévez, ranked 1st in the league for aerial duels won at 82.4%, is another significant absence for Parma, potentially weakening their midfield battle. Como, meanwhile, will be without forward Assane Diao, who has scored 8 goals and ranks 2nd league-wide for fouls drawn per game at 3.3, which could impact their attacking threat. Defender Alberto Dossena’s absence may also affect Como’s backline stability. These absences could tilt the balance depending on how both teams adapt.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Stats
sections.text: Parma’s season stats reveal a balanced attack with 40 goals (11th in the league), though they’ve conceded 53 goals (16th for least goals conceded). They excel in fast breaks, ranking 2nd league-wide with 1.29 per game, and have hit the woodwork 13 times, ranking 5th for unluckiest teams. Their goal distribution shows 14 goals (24.5%) in the 76-90 minute period, suggesting late-game resilience. Como’s goal distribution leans towards the second half, with 13 goals (16.7%) between 46-60 minutes and 9 goals (29.2%) in the final 15 minutes. With Parma averaging 4.53 corners per game (10th) and committing 353 fouls (20th for most fouls), expect a scrappy game with set-piece opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Players in Form
sections.text: Parma’s goalkeeper Zion Suzuki has been in excellent form over the last 5 matches, averaging a Sofascore rating of 7.5, which could be crucial in keeping Como’s attack at bay. Como’s goalkeeper Jean Butez has been even more impressive, averaging a 7.6 rating over the same period, suggesting a potential battle of the keepers. In the outfield, Parma’s Milan Đurić has contributed 4 goals and 1 assist, while Como’s Lucas Da Cunha offers flair with 3 goals and 2 assists. These in-form players could provide the decisive edge in a match expected to be closely contested.
